meta.title: Consistent Character Voices Using ONLY Text Prompts (Kling 3, Veo, Grok, WAN)
meta.description:
Creating consistent character voices in AI video is one of the trickiest parts of AI filmmaking. In this tutorial, I break down a simple 5-part voice prompting formula (Age, Gender, Timbre, Tone, and Pacing) that helps you get more consistent voices directly in your AI video generations, no extra tools required. === The cut and paste prompt is below! ===
I tested this method across Veo 3.1, Kling 3, LTX Pro, WAN 2.6, and Grok (which is free!) using a 3-character scene set aboard a space mining ship. I'll show you the results from each model and share which ones gave the best and most consistent output.
This isn't a perfect solution — but it's a "close enough" method you can use right now just by adding a few things to your prompt. 

🎬 AI Video School
https://aivideoschool.xyz/ai-video-school/consistent-ai-voices-text-prompts/

🛠️ AI TOOLS (some links are affiliate links — they help support the channel)
1️⃣ Kling AI: https://klingaiaffiliate.pxf.io/jeBGnM (Kling 3 is genuinely amazing)
2️⃣ Leonardo AI: https://leonardoai.pxf.io/POPYzQ (I used this for Veo and LTX)
3️⃣ Grok: https://grok.com/imagine (free! as of 2/12)
4️⃣ WAN: https://create.wan.video/ 

🗣️ Watch my other AI voice video about using ElevenLabs for cinematic AI movies:
https://youtu.be/fDk3wPhr_Ko
ElevenLabs: https://try.elevenlabs.io/lcol016s31sw   

📬 Sign up for my newsletter and get my free AI Video Prompt Sidekick for ChatGPT + updates on AI filmmaking courses
https://aivideoschool.substack.com/ 

🍿 AI Movies and How I Made Them
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLZrJsckCh5RzcTWyiaBqQ-zSGE-U0FsAg 

🎓 AI Video Tutorials, Comparisons, Workflows
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLZrJsckCh5RwGHkhWM7SB6-FFycNdagTF 

Give this to your favorite AI assistant and ask it to write voice prompts:
======
Voice Prompt Formula for Consistent AI Voices
To create consistent character voices across multiple shots, use this format:
He/She says in the voice of a [AGE] [GENDER], [TIMBRE], [TONE], [PACING]: "dialogue"
The Five Essential Elements
1. AGE - Approximate age range
    - Examples: young, middle-aged, elderly, teenage, mature
2. GENDER - Voice register
    - Examples: man, woman, boy, girl
3. TIMBRE - The physical quality of the voice
    - Examples: deep gentle voice, warm measured voice, sharp clear voice, bright voice, gravelly voice, smooth voice
4. TONE - The emotional quality or attitude
    - Examples: gentle tone, clinical tone, matter-of-fact tone, urgent whisper, concerned tone, confident tone
5. PACING - How fast or slow they speak
    - Examples: slow thoughtful pacing, deliberate pacing, moderate pacing, faster pacing, measured pacing
Pro Tips:
- Keep AGE, GENDER, and TIMBRE the same across all shots for each character (this is their "voice signature")
- Vary TONE and PACING based on emotion (angry = faster, sad = slower, etc.)
======

00:00 The problem with inconsistent AI character voices
01:23 How does this text prompt method for consistent voices work?
02:13 Can you include accents in text to voice prompts?
02:51 What are the elements of a good AI voice prompt?
03:25 Results: Veo 3.1
04:19 Results: WAN 2.6
05:08 Results: LTX 2 Pro
06:02 Results: Grok
06:48 Results: Kling 3
07:34 One more thing

#kling3 #klingai #veo3 #grok #wanai #ltx #cinematicai #aifilmmaking #consistentcharacter #aivoice #aivoices

transcript.text: One of the hardest parts of AI film
making is voice consistency. It sounds
pretty bad when a character talks like
this.
>> No one can know about this. It's too
dangerous.
>> And then a few seconds later like this.
>> I don't have the equipment to do a
proper autopsy.
>> That vocal mismatch takes the audience
out of the scene and they remember
they're watching an AI movie. While the
best results often require using
multiple tools and additional editing,
sometimes you're okay with a method
that's close enough and can be done at
the same time as generating your video.
>> Close enough.
>> How would someone smuggle poison on this
ship? I wouldn't say that too loud right
now if I were you. It means no one on
this ship is safe until we figure out
who killed Mike.
>> Close enough. So, to be clear, this
method does not result in perfect
results every time, but it should help
you get more consistent voices simply by
adding a few things to your prompt. I've

used that allow native audio, and I'll
show you those results in a minute.
We're going to try this out using a

and we'll hear each of them speak twice.

prediction engine. So, if you simply

you want the character to have.

>> So, what about accents? I tried using
